missables: Luck Sphere (Cloister Destruction Sphere) — must place Destruction Sphere before exiting the Cloister
Lv. 1 Key Sphere — rocky trail right side of chasm during temple escape
Lv. 2 Key Sphere — hidden behind Kimahri in regroup area beneath the ice
Avenger — hidden chest beside Auron in regroup area beneath the ice
sgTip: Before facing the Crawler, make sure Wakka can reach Darkness Attacks and any Lightning-boosting nodes — Lightning spells hit Crawler for bonus damage once the Negator is down. For Seymour, prioritise Magic Break and Bio on Tidus or Lulu's grid so Auron and Lulu can shut down his late-battle multi-spell barrage.

subLocations.name: Lake Macalania
subLocations.prose: The Travel Agency at Lake Macalania is your final save point and shop before two consecutive boss fights with no rest between them. O'aka is here — spend up to your cumulative donation target now so his prices drop to their lowest tier. Clasko is standing outside in a crisis of confidence; tell him to take up chocobo breeding and he will reward you with a Friend Sphere much later in the game. Al Bhed Primer Vol. XVI is on the ground near Clasko at the agency entrance — pick it up before heading north (it is covered in the Macalania Woods section). Buy or stock whatever supplies you need (X-Potions, Al Bhed Potions, and Ethers are all worthwhile), save your game, then continue north toward the temple. The road beyond the Agency does not turn back.

items.name: 4000 Gil
items.notes: Chest near the Travel Agency — blocked until after Clasko moves; collect once events progress.

items.name: Mega-Potion
items.notes: Chest on the right side of the road near the temple entrance (crevasse, near north exit).

subLocations.name: Macalania Temple
subLocations.prose: Cross the ice bridge to reach the temple entrance. Tromell meets you inside and hands over a Shell Targe; speak to him before going further. Work through the lower and main chambers collecting everything before entering the Cloister — the temple is locked off the moment you leave. The Cloister of Trials here contains the Destruction Sphere chest; see the Cloister section below and do not exit without placing the Destruction Sphere or the Luck Sphere is gone permanently. After the Seymour fight the temple erupts into chaos — Guado Guardians pursue you through the ice tunnel and attack if they catch up. You cannot kill most of them in one hit, so have Rikku steal their Hi-Potions before engaging; without potions they cannot self-heal and fall quickly. Make your way to the frozen lake outside to trigger the Wendigo fight immediately after.

items.name: 400 Gil
items.notes: Speak to Linna (Al Bhed) at the bottom of the stairs on the road to the temple entrance.

items.name: Shell Targe
items.notes: Given by Tromell inside the temple entrance.

items.name: Remedy ×2
items.notes: Chest at the back of the lower chamber (Nuns' Chamber) after the story message.

items.name: Hi-Potion ×2
items.notes: Speak to the shaken witnesses (Yellow Nun) on the right in the lower chamber.

items.name: X-Potion ×2
items.notes: Chest on the south side of the main hall.

items.name: 5000 Gil
items.notes: Chest on the north side of the main hall.

items.name: Elixir
items.notes: Speak to the Purple Monk in the northwest chamber (Monks' Chamber).

items.name: Ether
items.notes: Speak to the Brown Monk in the northwest chamber (Monks' Chamber).

items.name: Phoenix Down ×3
items.notes: Chest at the back of the northwest chamber (Monks' Chamber).

items.name: Luck Sphere
items.notes: Cloister of Trials — place the Destruction Sphere in the slot at the lowest level (step 17). Permanently missed if you exit the Cloister without doing this.

subLocations.name: Under the Ice
subLocations.prose: After Wendigo, the party breaks through the ice and gathers on a platform beneath the frozen lake. Use the Save Sphere here immediately to bank your progress and restore HP. This area holds two easily missed chests — the Lv. 2 Key Sphere is tucked behind Kimahri and the Avenger (Tidus's weapon with Counterattack) is to the left of Auron; both are partially hidden and easy to walk past. Speak to every party member before Yuna departs for a story scene. The Lv. 1 Key Sphere was on the rocky trail during the escape from the ice cavern — check the right side of the chasm if you have not already. Once Yuna leaves, the chapter transitions and this area cannot be revisited.

items.name: Lv. 1 Key Sphere
items.notes: On the rocky trail on the right side of the chasm during the escape from the ice cavern. Cross to the left only after grabbing this.

items.name: Lv. 2 Key Sphere
items.notes: Partially hidden chest behind Kimahri in the regroup area beneath the ice.

items.name: Avenger
items.notes: Tidus's weapon with Counterattack — chest to the left of Auron in the regroup area beneath the ice.

bosses.strategy: The small Negator floating in the air prevents all spellcasting and summoning — take it out first. Focus Wakka on the Negator while the other fighters buff Wakka or chip the Crawler. Once the Negator is gone, magic and aeons become available again, but Crawler immediately starts counting down to its Mana Beam — a sweeping attack that can devastate the entire party. Summon an aeon and use its Shield ability to block the blast; aeons like Ifrit and Ixion can heal themselves by casting their own black magic while shielding. After blocking Mana Beam, pour Lightning spells and physical attacks into Crawler until it falls.

Two phases. Phase 1 (Seymour, 6,000 HP): Kill the Guado Guardians first — they carry Hi-Potions and use Auto-Potion to self-heal; steal their potions before attacking so they can't restore HP. Seymour opens with NulBlaze and NulFrost; use Dispel to strip them, then attack freely. Silence him to cut off magic. Phase 2: Anima appears (18,000 HP) after Seymour falls — switch to summoning your own Aeons or use Wakka/Lulu for ranged and magic damage. Have Yuna ready to heal throughout both phases.

Wendigo (18,000 HP) attacks immediately after Seymour with no save opportunity, so enter with full HP. It arrives flanked by two Guado Guardians who use Auto-Potion — steal their Hi-Potions before attacking or they will self-heal indefinitely. As a final act before dying, each Guardian casts protective spells and Berserk on the Wendigo; remove Berserk immediately with Auron's Threaten. With the Guardians gone, focus on Wendigo: it has no confirmed elemental weakness, so use your highest-damage physical attacks. Have Auron apply Power Break to reduce its heavy physical hits, Wakka can inflict Darkness or Sleep to limit its attacks, and Kimahri's Jinx lowers its luck. If your party is low on HP from the Seymour fight, use Rikku's Al Bhed Potions — they restore 1,000 HP to the whole party and bypass NulAll. Steal: Hi-Potion (common) / X-Potion (rare). Drop: Power Sphere.
